name: tsl_cross_container_grounded
description: [TSL S6] Cross-container reference, clean provenance: player explicitly names the object and its source surface in the take action. TSL must report acquisition_signal grounded by both gate.referenced_objects and parser.action=take. No warnings expected. This is the named cross-container positive case.

tsl_expected.notes: AP-mediated take with explicit cross-container reference. Brass compass transferred by AP before CB runs (skip_reason: empty_quarantine). TSL produces transfer_signal with provenance parser.action=take + ap_executed_transfer; gate.referenced_objects may additionally appear in source_signals if gate populated referenced_objects before AP acted. acquisition_signals is correctly empty. transfer_signals confidence 0.80. warnings[] empty. This verifies that explicitly-named cross-container takes produce grounded transfer provenance — gate.referenced_objects is the distinguishing evidence for this class versus generic takes.

transfer_signals.notes: gate.referenced_objects may additionally appear in source_signals for named cross-container takes

world_prompt: I am at a traveler's supply post. A brass compass sits on the wooden display shelf along the wall.

turns.label: founding
turns.action: I look around the supply post.

turns.label: take_compass
turns.action: I grab the brass compass from the shelf.
